page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a compute-focused server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 360 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with the server achieving top-tier performance in PassMark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling into the average category, though Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reach top-tier levels. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which contribute to strong performance in cryptography, image processing, and LLM inference tasks. Specifically, prompt processing for small and medium LLM models ranks in the top tier and strong categories respectively, though text generation for large 70B models drops to average performance.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-standard-96 offers high compute density with a balanced memory allocation, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel processing workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached storage and the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. This configuration is suitable for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a compute-focused instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C x86_64 processor. The system provides 48 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 260 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. It contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, static web serving throughput, and small-model LLM prompt processing. However, single-core stress-ng performance and large-model text generation remain average. Lacking local storage, it relies entirely on network storage. This instance is designed for parallelized workloads, including software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 is a compute-focused instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and web serving performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-96 features 96 vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for multi-core compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 360 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, static web serving, and cryptography benchmark results
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
